[[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}
[[sn:Nukata clan]] [[ja:額田部氏]]
==Merged second translation== '''Nukatabe clan''' (ぬかたべうじ) refers to an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. It derives its name from the {{ill|name‐bearer|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name‐bearer|qq=}} of {{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Nukatabe Ren clan claims descent from {{ill|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} (referred to in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as Amatsuhiko no Mikoto) and is recorded in the Sakyō Shinbetsu of the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]] as “descendants of the same deity, following '''Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto'''.” This deity is one of the gods born from the [[:en:Ukehi|union]] between [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Susanoo‐no‐Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo‐no‐Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, and in volume one of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is regarded as the ancestral deity of {{ill|Bonkawachi clan|ja|凡河内氏|lt=Bonkawachi Naotachi|lt_ja=凡川内直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonkawachi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamashirokuni‐zo|ja|山背国造|lt=Yamashiro Naotachi|lt_ja=山代直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashirokuni‐zo|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], they are said to share the same ancestry as {{ill|Nukatabe Yudaren|ja|額田部湯坐連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Yudaren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the Sakyō Shinbetsu of the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]], others such as {{ill|Nukatabe Kataren|ja|額田部河田連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Kataren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also listed as their descendants.
Some theories suggest that the establishment of the clan as a distinct division may have been influenced by the rivalry between Yamato and Izumo. {{See|額田部}}
They established their stronghold in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] {{ill|Hiragorikun|ja|平群郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiragorikun|qq=}} Nukata District (currently [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamato‐Koriyama]]), and their family temple was [[:en:Kakuan-ji|Kakuan‐ji]]. The “Yamato Province Nukataji Garan Heijō Rizu” (housed in the National Museum of Japanese History and Folklore) is one of the few extant estate maps from the Nara period; it depicts the temple complex and its lands, and indicates that in the 8th century the Nukatabe clan, who venerated Nukataji as their family temple, simultaneously enshrined kofun as the clan’s “ancestral” tombs.
== [[:en:Okadayama Kofun|Okadayama Kofun]] Excavated Iron Sword (Izumo’s Nukatabe clan) == In the “Izumo Province Fudoki”, compiled in 733 ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 5), the term “Nukatabe Omi” appears in the entry for [[:en:Ohara District, Shimane|Oharagun]] as “{{ill|Shōryō|ja|少領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司}} lower‐conduct Rank, eighth level”.<ref>『Izumo Province 風土記』大原郡通道条</ref> Moreover,
{{quotation|The institute of the new establishment (Shinzau), a single location. It is situated in the village of Yaura. It is 11 ri and 120 steps directly north of the county seat. A three‐storied tower was constructed. There was one monk. It was built by the former Shōryō, Nukatabe Omi Oshishima. The current Shōryō, Ikomi, is his cousin.<ref>『Izumo Province 風土記』大原郡新造の院一所条</ref>}}
Thus, in the village of Yaura—the center of Oharagun (present‐day [[:en:Unnan|Unnan‐shi]])—there existed a temple said to have been established by the former Shōryō, Nukatabe Omi. From this it is apparent that the Nukatabe Omi clan was based in Oharagun. The administrators were often local aristocrats; in Izumo Province, not only the Nukatabe Omi but also figures such as {{ill|Takerube Omi|ja|建部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takerube Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hekibe Omi|ja|日置部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekibe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suguribe Omi|ja|勝部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguribe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Wabunbe Omi|ja|倭文部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wabunbe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—who bore the ministerial [[:en:Kabane|surname]]—are attested. This occurred because relatives of the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲氏|lt=Izumo Omi|lt_ja=出雲臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 出雲国造}} became administrators of local {{ill|section of subjects|ja|部民|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/section of subjects|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 部民制}}, adopting the ministerial surname.
The following archaeological discoveries are also of great importance in understanding the origins of the clan system and local administration, as well as the relationship between Izumo and Yamato.
On January 8, 1983 (Shōwa 58), during the conservation and repair of relics excavated from the Okadayama No.1 Kofun in {{ill|Okusa Town (Matsue)|ja|大草町 (松江市)|lt=Okusa Town|lt_ja=大草町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okusa Town (Matsue)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] (formerly Iu‐gun), which had been entrusted to the [[:en:Gangō-ji|Moto-ōji-ji]] Cultural Properties Research Institute, X‐ray imaging revealed that the blade of an iron sword (a large sword with a silver‐adorned, round head) bore an inlaid silver inscription of 12 characters. This kofun is located approximately one kilometer west of the site of the former government office ruins, close to the Izumo Province government office and the Iu‐gun family estate. It had been looted in 1915 ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 4), and the aforementioned iron sword was also discovered at that time. It was placed adjacent to the western side of a stone coffin, within a mortuary facility for secondary burial constructed from slab stones. Other items excavated simultaneously include a mirror bearing the “Chōshison” inscription, three large swords, sword hilts, {{ill|Tetsuzoku|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuzoku|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, a bronze and copper spherical bead, horse fittings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Additionally, a 1970 survey (Shōwa 45) identified numerous fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|Komochi Tsubo|ja|子持壺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komochi Tsubo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mound.
The large sword, as excavated, was complete; however, later half of the blade was lost, leaving only the final 12 characters of the inscription, and due to corrosion few legible characters remain. The inscription is as follows:
{{quotation|各田卩臣□□□素□大利□|}}
The term “各田卩臣” is believed to refer to “Nukatabe Omi” (ぬかたべのおみ).
As noted in the “Izumo Province Fudoki”, “Nukatabe Omi” can be seen as a local aristocrat of Oharagun. Furthermore, within the Izumo Omi clan it was not uncommon for subordinate ministers with different clan names, yet sharing the same surname, to exist—others include {{ill|Takerube Omi|ja|建部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takerube Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suguribe Omi|ja|勝部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguribe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kibibe Omi|ja|吉備部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibibe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. From this it is conceivable that Nukatabe Omi was a member of the Izumo Omi clan and based in Iu‐gun. The iron sword is thought to have been bestowed with an inscription carved with the name of one of the associates of the Nukatabe in the latter half of the 6th century by the Yamato regime, suggesting that Izumo existed under different political structures in its eastern and western regions.
Furthermore, at that time this region was the stronghold of the aristocratic Nukatabe clan, and Nukataji served as their family temple. The map depicts numerous tombs—including one annotated “Ship Tomb – Nukatabe Shukunē Ancestors”—which can still be verified on site and are known to be kofun dating from the 5th to 7th centuries. From the map it is evident that in the 8th century the Nukatabe clan, who revered Nukataji as their family temple, concurrently enshrined kofun as the ancestral tombs of the clan. The map thus reveals both a shift in the clan’s objects of veneration—from kofun to the family temple—and the diversity of venues for clan consolidation.
It is also said that in Izumo Province there existed both the Nukatabe chief family and a branch with no surname.
== Nukatabe Ren (Sukune) Clan == In volume 19 of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]],
{{quotation|In the 22nd year, Silla dispatched Kureshi and Kifubakkan, and tribute was presented. The number of courtesies offered by the attendant officials was always diminishing. Kifubakkan, indignant and resentful, departed.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇22nd year 条</ref>
(In the 22nd year, Silla dispatched Kureshi and Kyūbakkan, and tribute was rendered. As the manner of reception by the host officials was inferior to the standard, Kifubakkan, indignant, departed.) Translation: Ujitani Mou}}
Following this incident, in the same year,
{{quotation|In that year, again, Nu‐te‐da Sa was dispatched, and the previous tribute was rendered. In Naniwa’s great county, when various envoys were being arranged, the chief attendant, Nukatabe Ren, along with {{ill|Kazuraki Naotaira|ja|葛城国造|lt=Kazuraki Naotaira|lt_ja=葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Naotaira|qq=}}, among others, aligned under the command of Paekche, leading them forth. Da Sa, angered, returned. He did not enter the guest hall but boarded a ship and returned via the Anato (pass). (Text omitted thereafter.)<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇22nd year 是歳条</ref>
(In that same year, once again, Nu‐te‐da Sa was dispatched and the previous tribute rendered. In Naniwa’s great county (possibly the reception office), when envoys from various countries were being escorted, the reception officer, Nukatabe Ren, and {{ill|Kazuraki Naotaira|ja|葛城国造|lt=Kazuraki Naotaira|lt_ja=葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Naotaira|qq=}}, became upset because Naniwa was placed after Paekche, and thus Da Sa left in anger—opting to depart by ship via the Anato (also known as Nagato). (Text omitted thereafter.)) Translation: Ujitani Mou}}
This is regarded as the first recorded appearance of the Nukatabe clan in the literature. Emperor Kinmei—the father of Empress Suiko (born in his 15th year, 554)—and if Nukatabe served as Suiko’s {{ill|name‐bearer|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name‐bearer|qq=}}, then the Nukatabe clan was already in charge of the Nukatabe domain, which is consistent.
One year later, it is recorded that Silla destroyed the Mimana government establishment.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇23rd year 1月条</ref>
Furthermore, according to volume 22 of the Shoki and the [[:en:Book of Sui|Suishu]], in 608 during Empress Suiko’s reign, {{ill|Nukatabe Hibarobu|ja|額田部比羅夫|lt=Nukatabe Ren Hibarobu|lt_ja=額田部連比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hibarobu|qq=}} (Nukatabe no Muraji Hibarobu) welcomed the Sui envoy, Pei Seisei ([[:en:Pei Shiqing|Pei Seisei]]), at {{ill|Kaiseikyūshi|ja|海柘榴市 (Yamato Province)|lt=Kaiseikyūshi|lt_ja=海石榴市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiseikyūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tsubakichi) (present‐day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai‐shi]], Kanaya), and delivered a formal greeting (“words of courtesy”).
In 610, Nukatabe Hibarobu also welcomed envoys from Silla and Mimana, and, together with {{ill|Zen Omi Daitomo|ja|膳大伴|lt=Zen Omi Daitomo|lt_ja=膳臣大伴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zen Omi Daitomo|qq=}} (Kashiwade no Omi Ōtomo), he served as the head of Zōma (Kazariuma). In the following year, 611, during the medicinal animal hunting (kusurigari = young deer antlers, bagged horns) in Utano, he, together with {{ill|Awata Hosomem Omi|ja|粟田細目|lt=Awata Hosomem Omi|lt_ja=粟田細目臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata Hosomem Omi|qq=}}, served as the local administrator (kotori, meaning commander).<ref>『日本書紀』推古天皇19th year 5月5日条</ref>
In 645 (the first year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), Nukatabe Ren’s nephew became {{ill|Hōtō|ja|法頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtō|qq=}}.<ref>『日本書紀』孝徳天皇 大化元年8月8日条</ref>
The Nukatabe Ren clan, during the time of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in 684, obtained the title of {{ill|Yatsuiro no Sei|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsuiro no Sei|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} and the ministerial [[:en:Kabane|surname]] of Sukune.<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref> Furthermore, according to volume 1 of the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], a descendant believed to be {{ill|Nukatabe Hayashi|ja|額田部林|lt=Nukatabe Ren Hayashi|lt_ja=額田部連林|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hayashi|qq=}} (Nukatabe no Muraji Hayashi) participated in the compilation of the Taihō Code.<ref>『続日本紀』文武天皇4th year 6月17日条</ref>
== Nukatabe Clan of Nagato Province == They are a family of the {{ill|Anamono Kuni‐zukuri|ja|穴門国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamono Kuni‐zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊浦郡}}.
According to volume 13 of the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], in 740 (the 12th year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]]) during the rebellion led by {{ill|Fujiwara Hirosi|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hirosi|qq=}}, on September 21 an unsigned individual from the [[:en:Toyoura District, Yamaguchi|Toyoura District]] of Nagato Province, holding the Rank “Gai‐Sei Hachii jō”, namely {{ill|Nukatabe Hiromaro|ja|額田部広麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hiromaro|qq=}}, led 40 elite soldiers, crossed the sea, and landed in Kyushu.<ref>『続日本紀』聖武天皇 天平十二年9月24日条</ref> According to volume 14 of the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 続日本紀}}, after the suppression of this rebellion, it appears that he was granted the surname {{ill|Naotachi|ja|直|lt=Naotachi|lt_ja=直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naotachi|qq=}} and was promoted from the Rank of “Gai‐Sei Hachii jō” to {{ill|Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|ja|外従五位下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外位}}.<ref name="#1">『続日本紀』聖武天皇 天平十三年閏3月5日条</ref> In 738 (the 10th year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]]), he departed for [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō‐kyō]] as the {{ill|Burenshi|ja|部領使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burenshi|qq=}} of the “Tanra” islanders ([[:en:Tamna|Tanra]]), passing through {{ill|Suō Province|ja|Suō Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along the way.
According to volume 28 of the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 続日本紀}}, in April 767 (the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō-jingo|Tenpyō Shingogō]]), {{ill|Nukatabe Sekimori|ja|額田部塞守|lt=Nukatabe Naosekimori|lt_ja=額田部直塞守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Sekimori|qq=}}, holding the Rank “Gai‐chū Shichii jō” and serving as the commander of the Toyoura group in Nagato Province, presented 1,000,000 mon in coin and 10,000 bundles of rice, for which he was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|ja|外従五位下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外位}} and appointed as the great administrator of Toyoura District.<ref>『続日本紀』称徳天皇 天平神護3rd year 4月29日条</ref>
== Nukatabe Yudaren and Nukatabe Kataren == In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]], two ancestral traditions of the Nukatabe clan are recorded as follows:
* During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Yungyō]], a descendant of {{ill|Amatsumikage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsumikage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神}} was sent to {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and subdued the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayabito]]. Upon his return, he presented a single horse; and because the horse’s forehead bore hair in the shape of a paddy field, the emperor was pleased and granted him the surname of {{ill|Nukatabe Yudaren|ja|額田部湯坐連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Yudaren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『新撰姓氏録』允恭天皇御世。被遣Satsuma Province 。平隼人。復奏之日。献御馬一匹。額有町形廻毛。天皇嘉之。賜姓額田部湯坐連也。</ref>
* During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Yungyō]], a descendant of the third‐generation descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}, namely {{ill|Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto|ja|意富伊我都命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, presented a horse whose forehead bore hair in the shape of a rice paddy; consequently, he was granted the surname of {{ill|Nukatabe Kataren|ja|額田部河田連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Kataren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『新撰姓氏録』允恭天皇御世。献額田馬。天皇勅。此馬額如田町。仍賜姓額田部河田連也。</ref>
== Other Nukatabe Clans == In regional branches of the Nukatabe, {{ill|Nukatabe Soteme|ja|額田部蘇提売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Soteme|qq=}} (ぬかたべ の そてめ), who was honored in February of the 2nd year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] (768) under Emperor Shōtoku, is well known.<ref name="#1"/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55532736]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nukatabe Clan''' (額田部氏) was an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]], originating from the {{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}} organization under the {{ill|Me|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Me|qq=|1=Me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}} system.
== Overview == === Ancestry === The Nukatabe Ren clan claimed descent from {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} (referred to as Amatsuhikone no Mikoto in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]). According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (Left Capital, Kami Clan section), they were descendants of "Otowiigatsu no Mikoto" (意富伊我都命). This deity was one of those born from the [[:en:Ukehi|ukehi]] ritual between [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, as described in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1. Additionally, the clan is mentioned as an ancestor of the {{ill|Onokuchi no Miyatsuko|ja|凡河内氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokuchi no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395444}} and {{ill|Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|ja|山背国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470858}}.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamiyo Volume 1, Sixth Section.</ref> The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] also associates the Nukatabe clan with the Nukatabe Yuzaka Ren and others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
A theory suggests the clan's establishment reflects conflicts between the Yamato and Izumo regions.{{See|{{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}}}}
=== Base of Operations === The clan's base was located in Nukata Village, {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|qq=|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}}, Yamato Province (modern [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]]), where they built their {{ill|ujidera|ja|氏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ujidera|qq=|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}, [[:en:Kakuan-ji|Kakuan-ji]]. The preserved "Yamato Province Nukata-ji Temple Site and Strip Village Map" at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] provides a rare depiction of temple layouts and estates during the Nara period, revealing the clan’s faith and burial practices.
== Archaeological Evidence == === Izumo Nukatabe Clan === The [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]] of {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the Nukatabe no Omi family in its records on [[:en:Ohara District, Shimane|Ōhara District]]. One notable entry describes a previous {{ill|Shōryō|ja|少領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}}, Nukatabe no Omi Oshishima, who built a temple.<ref>{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ōhara District, Tsūji Section.</ref>
A significant archaeological discovery related to this clan is the iron sword with silver-inlaid inscriptions found in the Okadayama Kofun (Matsue City, Shimane Prefecture). This sword, discovered during preservation work in 1983, bears the inscription "各田卩臣," interpreted as "Nukatabe no Omi." It suggests a connection to local governance during the late 6th century.
=== Other Artifacts === Other artifacts from the Okadayama Kofun include mirrors, weapons, and horse gear, reflecting the clan's social and political significance. These findings support the theory that Nukatabe no Omi managed local estates as part of the Yamato government’s administrative system.
== Key Figures == * '''Nukatabe Hirafu''' (額田部比羅夫): Notable as a diplomat under Empress Suiko, Hirafu welcomed the Sui envoy, Pei Shiqing, at Tsubaki Market and performed ceremonial duties.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Empress Suiko, Year 16.</ref> * '''Nukatabe no Omi Rei''' (額田部連甥): Appointed as {{ill|Hōtō|ja|法頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtō|qq=|1=Hōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272314}} during Emperor Kōtoku's reign (645 CE).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku, Year 1.</ref>
== Nukatabe Ren and Nukatabe Kawada Ren == The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] includes two origin stories: * Nukatabe Yuzaka Ren received their surname after presenting a horse with a field-shaped marking to Emperor Ingyō. * Nukatabe Kawada Ren also earned their name for offering a similarly marked horse.<ref>[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Emperor Ingyō Section.</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Bemin|Be System]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|額田部氏|version=96247839|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}
[[sn:Nukata clan]] [[ja:額田部氏]]
==Merged second translation== '''Nukatabe clan''' (ぬかたべうじ) refers to an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. It derives its name from the {{ill|name‐bearer|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name‐bearer|qq=|1=name‐bearer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}} of {{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}}.
== Overview == The Nukatabe Ren clan claims descent from {{ill|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} (referred to in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as Amatsuhiko no Mikoto) and is recorded in the Sakyō Shinbetsu of the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]] as “descendants of the same deity, following '''Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto'''.” This deity is one of the gods born from the [[:en:Ukehi|union]] between [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Susanoo‐no‐Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo‐no‐Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|1=Susanoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q272993}}, and in volume one of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is regarded as the ancestral deity of {{ill|Bonkawachi clan|ja|凡河内氏|lt=Bonkawachi Naotachi|lt_ja=凡川内直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonkawachi clan|qq=|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395444}} and {{ill|Yamashirokuni‐zo|ja|山背国造|lt=Yamashiro Naotachi|lt_ja=山代直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashirokuni‐zo|qq=|1=Yamashirokuni‐zo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470858}}. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], they are said to share the same ancestry as {{ill|Nukatabe Yudaren|ja|額田部湯坐連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Yudaren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the Sakyō Shinbetsu of the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]], others such as {{ill|Nukatabe Kataren|ja|額田部河田連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Kataren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also listed as their descendants.
Some theories suggest that the establishment of the clan as a distinct division may have been influenced by the rivalry between Yamato and Izumo. {{See|額田部}}
They established their stronghold in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] {{ill|Hiragorikun|ja|平群郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiragorikun|qq=|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}} Nukata District (currently [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamato‐Koriyama]]), and their family temple was [[:en:Kakuan-ji|Kakuan‐ji]]. The “Yamato Province Nukataji Garan Heijō Rizu” (housed in the National Museum of Japanese History and Folklore) is one of the few extant estate maps from the Nara period; it depicts the temple complex and its lands, and indicates that in the 8th century the Nukatabe clan, who venerated Nukataji as their family temple, simultaneously enshrined kofun as the clan’s “ancestral” tombs.
== [[:en:Okadayama Kofun|Okadayama Kofun]] Excavated Iron Sword (Izumo’s Nukatabe clan) == In the “Izumo Province Fudoki”, compiled in 733 ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 5), the term “Nukatabe Omi” appears in the entry for [[:en:Ohara District, Shimane|Oharagun]] as “{{ill|Shōryō|ja|少領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}} lower‐conduct Rank, eighth level”.<ref>『Izumo Province 風土記』大原郡通道条</ref> Moreover,
{{quotation|The institute of the new establishment (Shinzau), a single location. It is situated in the village of Yaura. It is 11 ri and 120 steps directly north of the county seat. A three‐storied tower was constructed. There was one monk. It was built by the former Shōryō, Nukatabe Omi Oshishima. The current Shōryō, Ikomi, is his cousin.<ref>『Izumo Province 風土記』大原郡新造の院一所条</ref>}}
Thus, in the village of Yaura—the center of Oharagun (present‐day [[:en:Unnan|Unnan‐shi]])—there existed a temple said to have been established by the former Shōryō, Nukatabe Omi. From this it is apparent that the Nukatabe Omi clan was based in Oharagun. The administrators were often local aristocrats; in Izumo Province, not only the Nukatabe Omi but also figures such as {{ill|Takerube Omi|ja|建部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takerube Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hekibe Omi|ja|日置部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekibe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suguribe Omi|ja|勝部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguribe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Wabunbe Omi|ja|倭文部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wabunbe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—who bore the ministerial [[:en:Kabane|surname]]—are attested. This occurred because relatives of the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲氏|lt=Izumo Omi|lt_ja=出雲臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 出雲国造}} became administrators of local {{ill|section of subjects|ja|部民|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/section of subjects|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 部民制|1=bemin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2892644}}, adopting the ministerial surname.
The following archaeological discoveries are also of great importance in understanding the origins of the clan system and local administration, as well as the relationship between Izumo and Yamato.
On January 8, 1983 (Shōwa 58), during the conservation and repair of relics excavated from the Okadayama No.1 Kofun in {{ill|Okusa Town (Matsue)|ja|大草町 (松江市)|lt=Okusa Town|lt_ja=大草町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okusa Town (Matsue)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] (formerly Iu‐gun), which had been entrusted to the [[:en:Gangō-ji|Moto-ōji-ji]] Cultural Properties Research Institute, X‐ray imaging revealed that the blade of an iron sword (a large sword with a silver‐adorned, round head) bore an inlaid silver inscription of 12 characters. This kofun is located approximately one kilometer west of the site of the former government office ruins, close to the Izumo Province government office and the Iu‐gun family estate. It had been looted in 1915 ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 4), and the aforementioned iron sword was also discovered at that time. It was placed adjacent to the western side of a stone coffin, within a mortuary facility for secondary burial constructed from slab stones. Other items excavated simultaneously include a mirror bearing the “Chōshison” inscription, three large swords, sword hilts, {{ill|Tetsuzoku|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuzoku|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, a bronze and copper spherical bead, horse fittings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Additionally, a 1970 survey (Shōwa 45) identified numerous fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|qq=|1=cylindrical haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394845}} and {{ill|Komochi Tsubo|ja|子持壺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komochi Tsubo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mound.
The large sword, as excavated, was complete; however, later half of the blade was lost, leaving only the final 12 characters of the inscription, and due to corrosion few legible characters remain. The inscription is as follows:
{{quotation|各田卩臣□□□素□大利□|}}
The term “各田卩臣” is believed to refer to “Nukatabe Omi” (ぬかたべのおみ).
As noted in the “Izumo Province Fudoki”, “Nukatabe Omi” can be seen as a local aristocrat of Oharagun. Furthermore, within the Izumo Omi clan it was not uncommon for subordinate ministers with different clan names, yet sharing the same surname, to exist—others include {{ill|Takerube Omi|ja|建部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takerube Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suguribe Omi|ja|勝部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguribe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kibibe Omi|ja|吉備部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibibe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. From this it is conceivable that Nukatabe Omi was a member of the Izumo Omi clan and based in Iu‐gun. The iron sword is thought to have been bestowed with an inscription carved with the name of one of the associates of the Nukatabe in the latter half of the 6th century by the Yamato regime, suggesting that Izumo existed under different political structures in its eastern and western regions.
Furthermore, at that time this region was the stronghold of the aristocratic Nukatabe clan, and Nukataji served as their family temple. The map depicts numerous tombs—including one annotated “Ship Tomb – Nukatabe Shukunē Ancestors”—which can still be verified on site and are known to be kofun dating from the 5th to 7th centuries. From the map it is evident that in the 8th century the Nukatabe clan, who revered Nukataji as their family temple, concurrently enshrined kofun as the ancestral tombs of the clan. The map thus reveals both a shift in the clan’s objects of veneration—from kofun to the family temple—and the diversity of venues for clan consolidation.
It is also said that in Izumo Province there existed both the Nukatabe chief family and a branch with no surname.
== Nukatabe Ren (Sukune) Clan == In volume 19 of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]],
{{quotation|In the 22nd year, Silla dispatched Kureshi and Kifubakkan, and tribute was presented. The number of courtesies offered by the attendant officials was always diminishing. Kifubakkan, indignant and resentful, departed.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇22nd year 条</ref>
(In the 22nd year, Silla dispatched Kureshi and Kyūbakkan, and tribute was rendered. As the manner of reception by the host officials was inferior to the standard, Kifubakkan, indignant, departed.) Translation: Ujitani Mou}}
Following this incident, in the same year,
{{quotation|In that year, again, Nu‐te‐da Sa was dispatched, and the previous tribute was rendered. In Naniwa’s great county, when various envoys were being arranged, the chief attendant, Nukatabe Ren, along with {{ill|Kazuraki Naotaira|ja|葛城国造|lt=Kazuraki Naotaira|lt_ja=葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Naotaira|qq=|1=Kazuraki Naotaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}}, among others, aligned under the command of Paekche, leading them forth. Da Sa, angered, returned. He did not enter the guest hall but boarded a ship and returned via the Anato (pass). (Text omitted thereafter.)<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇22nd year 是歳条</ref>
(In that same year, once again, Nu‐te‐da Sa was dispatched and the previous tribute rendered. In Naniwa’s great county (possibly the reception office), when envoys from various countries were being escorted, the reception officer, Nukatabe Ren, and {{ill|Kazuraki Naotaira|ja|葛城国造|lt=Kazuraki Naotaira|lt_ja=葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Naotaira|qq=|1=Kazuraki Naotaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}}, became upset because Naniwa was placed after Paekche, and thus Da Sa left in anger—opting to depart by ship via the Anato (also known as Nagato). (Text omitted thereafter.)) Translation: Ujitani Mou}}
This is regarded as the first recorded appearance of the Nukatabe clan in the literature. Emperor Kinmei—the father of Empress Suiko (born in his 15th year, 554)—and if Nukatabe served as Suiko’s {{ill|name‐bearer|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name‐bearer|qq=|1=name‐bearer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}}, then the Nukatabe clan was already in charge of the Nukatabe domain, which is consistent.
One year later, it is recorded that Silla destroyed the Mimana government establishment.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇23rd year 1月条</ref>
Furthermore, according to volume 22 of the Shoki and the [[:en:Book of Sui|Suishu]], in 608 during Empress Suiko’s reign, {{ill|Nukatabe Hibarobu|ja|額田部比羅夫|lt=Nukatabe Ren Hibarobu|lt_ja=額田部連比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hibarobu|qq=|1=Nukatabe Hibarobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55050985}} (Nukatabe no Muraji Hibarobu) welcomed the Sui envoy, Pei Seisei ([[:en:Pei Shiqing|Pei Seisei]]), at {{ill|Kaiseikyūshi|ja|海柘榴市 (Yamato Province)|lt=Kaiseikyūshi|lt_ja=海石榴市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiseikyūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tsubakichi) (present‐day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai‐shi]], Kanaya), and delivered a formal greeting (“words of courtesy”).
In 610, Nukatabe Hibarobu also welcomed envoys from Silla and Mimana, and, together with {{ill|Zen Omi Daitomo|ja|膳大伴|lt=Zen Omi Daitomo|lt_ja=膳臣大伴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zen Omi Daitomo|qq=|1=Zen Omi Daitomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536854}} (Kashiwade no Omi Ōtomo), he served as the head of Zōma (Kazariuma). In the following year, 611, during the medicinal animal hunting (kusurigari = young deer antlers, bagged horns) in Utano, he, together with {{ill|Awata Hosomem Omi|ja|粟田細目|lt=Awata Hosomem Omi|lt_ja=粟田細目臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata Hosomem Omi|qq=|1=Awata no Hosome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55050998}}, served as the local administrator (kotori, meaning commander).<ref>『日本書紀』推古天皇19th year 5月5日条</ref>
In 645 (the first year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), Nukatabe Ren’s nephew became {{ill|Hōtō|ja|法頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtō|qq=|1=Hōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272314}}.<ref>『日本書紀』孝徳天皇 大化元年8月8日条</ref>
The Nukatabe Ren clan, during the time of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in 684, obtained the title of {{ill|Yatsuiro no Sei|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsuiro no Sei|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} and the ministerial [[:en:Kabane|surname]] of Sukune.<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref> Furthermore, according to volume 1 of the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], a descendant believed to be {{ill|Nukatabe Hayashi|ja|額田部林|lt=Nukatabe Ren Hayashi|lt_ja=額田部連林|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hayashi|qq=|1=Nukatabe Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532738}} (Nukatabe no Muraji Hayashi) participated in the compilation of the Taihō Code.<ref>『続日本紀』文武天皇4th year 6月17日条</ref>
== Nukatabe Clan of Nagato Province == They are a family of the {{ill|Anamono Kuni‐zukuri|ja|穴門国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamono Kuni‐zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊浦郡|1=Toyoura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7830939}}.
According to volume 13 of the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], in 740 (the 12th year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]]) during the rebellion led by {{ill|Fujiwara Hirosi|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hirosi|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}}, on September 21 an unsigned individual from the [[:en:Toyoura District, Yamaguchi|Toyoura District]] of Nagato Province, holding the Rank “Gai‐Sei Hachii jō”, namely {{ill|Nukatabe Hiromaro|ja|額田部広麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hiromaro|qq=|1=Nukatabe Hiromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55050993}}, led 40 elite soldiers, crossed the sea, and landed in Kyushu.<ref>『続日本紀』聖武天皇 天平十二年9月24日条</ref> According to volume 14 of the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 続日本紀}}, after the suppression of this rebellion, it appears that he was granted the surname {{ill|Naotachi|ja|直|lt=Naotachi|lt_ja=直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naotachi|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}} and was promoted from the Rank of “Gai‐Sei Hachii jō” to {{ill|Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|ja|外従五位下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外位|1=Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430321}}.<ref name="#1">『続日本紀』聖武天皇 天平十三年閏3月5日条</ref> In 738 (the 10th year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]]), he departed for [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō‐kyō]] as the {{ill|Burenshi|ja|部領使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burenshi|qq=|1=Burenshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532745}} of the “Tanra” islanders ([[:en:Tamna|Tanra]]), passing through {{ill|Suō Province|ja|Suō Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along the way.
According to volume 28 of the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 続日本紀}}, in April 767 (the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō-jingo|Tenpyō Shingogō]]), {{ill|Nukatabe Sekimori|ja|額田部塞守|lt=Nukatabe Naosekimori|lt_ja=額田部直塞守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Sekimori|qq=|1=Nukatabe Sekimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406938}}, holding the Rank “Gai‐chū Shichii jō” and serving as the commander of the Toyoura group in Nagato Province, presented 1,000,000 mon in coin and 10,000 bundles of rice, for which he was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|ja|外従五位下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外位|1=Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430321}} and appointed as the great administrator of Toyoura District.<ref>『続日本紀』称徳天皇 天平神護3rd year 4月29日条</ref>
== Nukatabe Yudaren and Nukatabe Kataren == In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]], two ancestral traditions of the Nukatabe clan are recorded as follows:
* During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Yungyō]], a descendant of {{ill|Amatsumikage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsumikage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}} was sent to {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and subdued the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayabito]]. Upon his return, he presented a single horse; and because the horse’s forehead bore hair in the shape of a paddy field, the emperor was pleased and granted him the surname of {{ill|Nukatabe Yudaren|ja|額田部湯坐連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Yudaren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『新撰姓氏録』允恭天皇御世。被遣Satsuma Province 。平隼人。復奏之日。献御馬一匹。額有町形廻毛。天皇嘉之。賜姓額田部湯坐連也。</ref>
* During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Yungyō]], a descendant of the third‐generation descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}}, namely {{ill|Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto|ja|意富伊我都命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, presented a horse whose forehead bore hair in the shape of a rice paddy; consequently, he was granted the surname of {{ill|Nukatabe Kataren|ja|額田部河田連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Kataren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『新撰姓氏録』允恭天皇御世。献額田馬。天皇勅。此馬額如田町。仍賜姓額田部河田連也。</ref>
== Other Nukatabe Clans == In regional branches of the Nukatabe, {{ill|Nukatabe Soteme|ja|額田部蘇提売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Soteme|qq=|1=Nukatabe Soteme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532744}} (ぬかたべ の そてめ), who was honored in February of the 2nd year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] (768) under Emperor Shōtoku, is well known.<ref name="#1"/>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55532736]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nukatabe Clan''' (額田部氏) was an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]], originating from the {{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}} organization under the {{ill|Me|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Me|qq=|1=Me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}} system.
== Overview == === Ancestry === The Nukatabe Ren clan claimed descent from {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} (referred to as Amatsuhikone no Mikoto in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]). According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (Left Capital, Kami Clan section), they were descendants of "Otowiigatsu no Mikoto" (意富伊我都命). This deity was one of those born from the [[:en:Ukehi|ukehi]] ritual between [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, as described in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1. Additionally, the clan is mentioned as an ancestor of the {{ill|Onokuchi no Miyatsuko|ja|凡河内氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokuchi no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395444}} and {{ill|Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|ja|山背国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Yamashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470858}}.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamiyo Volume 1, Sixth Section.</ref> The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] also associates the Nukatabe clan with the Nukatabe Yuzaka Ren and others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
A theory suggests the clan's establishment reflects conflicts between the Yamato and Izumo regions.{{See|{{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}}}}
=== Base of Operations === The clan's base was located in Nukata Village, {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|qq=|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}}, Yamato Province (modern [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]]), where they built their {{ill|ujidera|ja|氏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ujidera|qq=|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}, [[:en:Kakuan-ji|Kakuan-ji]]. The preserved "Yamato Province Nukata-ji Temple Site and Strip Village Map" at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] provides a rare depiction of temple layouts and estates during the Nara period, revealing the clan’s faith and burial practices.
== Archaeological Evidence == === Izumo Nukatabe Clan === The [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]] of {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the Nukatabe no Omi family in its records on [[:en:Ohara District, Shimane|Ōhara District]]. One notable entry describes a previous {{ill|Shōryō|ja|少領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}}, Nukatabe no Omi Oshishima, who built a temple.<ref>{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ōhara District, Tsūji Section.</ref>
A significant archaeological discovery related to this clan is the iron sword with silver-inlaid inscriptions found in the Okadayama Kofun (Matsue City, Shimane Prefecture). This sword, discovered during preservation work in 1983, bears the inscription "各田卩臣," interpreted as "Nukatabe no Omi." It suggests a connection to local governance during the late 6th century.
=== Other Artifacts === Other artifacts from the Okadayama Kofun include mirrors, weapons, and horse gear, reflecting the clan's social and political significance. These findings support the theory that Nukatabe no Omi managed local estates as part of the Yamato government’s administrative system.
== Key Figures == * '''Nukatabe Hirafu''' (額田部比羅夫): Notable as a diplomat under Empress Suiko, Hirafu welcomed the Sui envoy, Pei Shiqing, at Tsubaki Market and performed ceremonial duties.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Empress Suiko, Year 16.</ref> * '''Nukatabe no Omi Rei''' (額田部連甥): Appointed as {{ill|Hōtō|ja|法頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtō|qq=|1=Hōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272314}} during Emperor Kōtoku's reign (645 CE).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku, Year 1.</ref>
== Nukatabe Ren and Nukatabe Kawada Ren == The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] includes two origin stories: * Nukatabe Yuzaka Ren received their surname after presenting a horse with a field-shaped marking to Emperor Ingyō. * Nukatabe Kawada Ren also earned their name for offering a similarly marked horse.<ref>[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Emperor Ingyō Section.</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Bemin|Be System]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|額田部氏|version=96247839|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 1]] }}
[[sn:Nukata clan]] [[ja:額田部氏]]
==Merged second translation== '''Nukatabe clan''' (ぬかたべうじ) refers to an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. It derives its name from the {{ill|name‐bearer|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name‐bearer|qq=|1=name‐bearer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}} of {{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}}.
== Overview == The Nukatabe Ren clan claims descent from {{ill|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}} (referred to in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as Amatsuhiko no Mikoto) and is recorded in the Sakyō Shinbetsu of the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]] as “descendants of the same deity, following '''Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto'''.” This deity is one of the gods born from the [[:en:Ukehi|union]] between [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Susanoo‐no‐Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo‐no‐Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|1=Susanoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q272993}}, and in volume one of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he is regarded as the ancestral deity of {{ill|Bonkawachi clan|ja|凡河内氏|lt=Bonkawachi Naotachi|lt_ja=凡川内直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bonkawachi clan|qq=|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395444}} and {{ill|Yamashirokuni‐zo|ja|山背国造|lt=Yamashiro Naotachi|lt_ja=山代直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashirokuni‐zo|qq=|1=Yamashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470858}}. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], they are said to share the same ancestry as {{ill|Nukatabe Yudaren|ja|額田部湯坐連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Yudaren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in the Sakyō Shinbetsu of the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]], others such as {{ill|Nukatabe Kataren|ja|額田部河田連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Kataren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are also listed as their descendants.
Some theories suggest that the establishment of the clan as a distinct division may have been influenced by the rivalry between Yamato and Izumo. {{See|額田部}}
They established their stronghold in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] {{ill|Hiragorikun|ja|平群郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiragorikun|qq=|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}} Nukata District (currently [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamato‐Koriyama]]), and their family temple was [[:en:Kakuan-ji|Kakuan‐ji]]. The “Yamato Province Nukataji Garan Heijō Rizu” (housed in the National Museum of Japanese History and Folklore) is one of the few extant estate maps from the Nara period; it depicts the temple complex and its lands, and indicates that in the 8th century the Nukatabe clan, who venerated Nukataji as their family temple, simultaneously enshrined kofun as the clan’s “ancestral” tombs.
== [[:en:Okadayama Kofun|Okadayama Kofun]] Excavated Iron Sword (Izumo’s Nukatabe clan) == In the “Izumo Province Fudoki”, compiled in 733 ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 5), the term “Nukatabe Omi” appears in the entry for [[:en:Ohara District, Shimane|Oharagun]] as “{{ill|Shōryō|ja|少領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}} lower‐conduct Rank, eighth level”.<ref>『Izumo Province 風土記』大原郡通道条</ref> Moreover,
{{quotation|The institute of the new establishment (Shinzau), a single location. It is situated in the village of Yaura. It is 11 ri and 120 steps directly north of the county seat. A three‐storied tower was constructed. There was one monk. It was built by the former Shōryō, Nukatabe Omi Oshishima. The current Shōryō, Ikomi, is his cousin.<ref>『Izumo Province 風土記』大原郡新造の院一所条</ref>}}
Thus, in the village of Yaura—the center of Oharagun (present‐day [[:en:Unnan|Unnan‐shi]])—there existed a temple said to have been established by the former Shōryō, Nukatabe Omi. From this it is apparent that the Nukatabe Omi clan was based in Oharagun. The administrators were often local aristocrats; in Izumo Province, not only the Nukatabe Omi but also figures such as {{ill|Takerube Omi|ja|建部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takerube Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hekibe Omi|ja|日置部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hekibe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suguribe Omi|ja|勝部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguribe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Wabunbe Omi|ja|倭文部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wabunbe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—who bore the ministerial [[:en:Kabane|surname]]—are attested. This occurred because relatives of the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲氏|lt=Izumo Omi|lt_ja=出雲臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 出雲国造}} became administrators of local {{ill|section of subjects|ja|部民|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/section of subjects|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 部民制|1=bemin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2892644}}, adopting the ministerial surname.
The following archaeological discoveries are also of great importance in understanding the origins of the clan system and local administration, as well as the relationship between Izumo and Yamato.
On January 8, 1983 (Shōwa 58), during the conservation and repair of relics excavated from the Okadayama No.1 Kofun in {{ill|Okusa Town (Matsue)|ja|大草町 (松江市)|lt=Okusa Town|lt_ja=大草町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okusa Town (Matsue)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] (formerly Iu‐gun), which had been entrusted to the [[:en:Gangō-ji|Moto-ōji-ji]] Cultural Properties Research Institute, X‐ray imaging revealed that the blade of an iron sword (a large sword with a silver‐adorned, round head) bore an inlaid silver inscription of 12 characters. This kofun is located approximately one kilometer west of the site of the former government office ruins, close to the Izumo Province government office and the Iu‐gun family estate. It had been looted in 1915 ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 4), and the aforementioned iron sword was also discovered at that time. It was placed adjacent to the western side of a stone coffin, within a mortuary facility for secondary burial constructed from slab stones. Other items excavated simultaneously include a mirror bearing the “Chōshison” inscription, three large swords, sword hilts, {{ill|Tetsuzoku|ja|鉄鏃|1=Iron arrowheads|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuzoku|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron arrowheads|qq=}}, a bronze and copper spherical bead, horse fittings, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. Additionally, a 1970 survey (Shōwa 45) identified numerous fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|qq=|1=cylindrical haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394845}} and {{ill|Komochi Tsubo|ja|子持壺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komochi Tsubo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mound.
The large sword, as excavated, was complete; however, later half of the blade was lost, leaving only the final 12 characters of the inscription, and due to corrosion few legible characters remain. The inscription is as follows:
{{quotation|各田卩臣□□□素□大利□|}}
The term “各田卩臣” is believed to refer to “Nukatabe Omi” (ぬかたべのおみ).
As noted in the “Izumo Province Fudoki”, “Nukatabe Omi” can be seen as a local aristocrat of Oharagun. Furthermore, within the Izumo Omi clan it was not uncommon for subordinate ministers with different clan names, yet sharing the same surname, to exist—others include {{ill|Takerube Omi|ja|建部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takerube Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Suguribe Omi|ja|勝部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suguribe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kibibe Omi|ja|吉備部臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibibe Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. From this it is conceivable that Nukatabe Omi was a member of the Izumo Omi clan and based in Iu‐gun. The iron sword is thought to have been bestowed with an inscription carved with the name of one of the associates of the Nukatabe in the latter half of the 6th century by the Yamato regime, suggesting that Izumo existed under different political structures in its eastern and western regions.
Furthermore, at that time this region was the stronghold of the aristocratic Nukatabe clan, and Nukataji served as their family temple. The map depicts numerous tombs—including one annotated “Ship Tomb – Nukatabe Shukunē Ancestors”—which can still be verified on site and are known to be kofun dating from the 5th to 7th centuries. From the map it is evident that in the 8th century the Nukatabe clan, who revered Nukataji as their family temple, concurrently enshrined kofun as the ancestral tombs of the clan. The map thus reveals both a shift in the clan’s objects of veneration—from kofun to the family temple—and the diversity of venues for clan consolidation.
It is also said that in Izumo Province there existed both the Nukatabe chief family and a branch with no surname.
== Nukatabe Ren (Sukune) Clan == In volume 19 of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]],
{{quotation|In the 22nd year, Silla dispatched Kureshi and Kifubakkan, and tribute was presented. The number of courtesies offered by the attendant officials was always diminishing. Kifubakkan, indignant and resentful, departed.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇22nd year 条</ref>
(In the 22nd year, Silla dispatched Kureshi and Kyūbakkan, and tribute was rendered. As the manner of reception by the host officials was inferior to the standard, Kifubakkan, indignant, departed.) Translation: Ujitani Mou}}
Following this incident, in the same year,
{{quotation|In that year, again, Nu‐te‐da Sa was dispatched, and the previous tribute was rendered. In Naniwa’s great county, when various envoys were being arranged, the chief attendant, Nukatabe Ren, along with {{ill|Kazuraki Naotaira|ja|葛城国造|lt=Kazuraki Naotaira|lt_ja=葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Naotaira|qq=|1=Kazuraki Naotaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}}, among others, aligned under the command of Paekche, leading them forth. Da Sa, angered, returned. He did not enter the guest hall but boarded a ship and returned via the Anato (pass). (Text omitted thereafter.)<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇22nd year 是歳条</ref>
(In that same year, once again, Nu‐te‐da Sa was dispatched and the previous tribute rendered. In Naniwa’s great county (possibly the reception office), when envoys from various countries were being escorted, the reception officer, Nukatabe Ren, and {{ill|Kazuraki Naotaira|ja|葛城国造|lt=Kazuraki Naotaira|lt_ja=葛城直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuraki Naotaira|qq=|1=Kazuraki Naotaira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620893}}, became upset because Naniwa was placed after Paekche, and thus Da Sa left in anger—opting to depart by ship via the Anato (also known as Nagato). (Text omitted thereafter.)) Translation: Ujitani Mou}}
This is regarded as the first recorded appearance of the Nukatabe clan in the literature. Emperor Kinmei—the father of Empress Suiko (born in his 15th year, 554)—and if Nukatabe served as Suiko’s {{ill|name‐bearer|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/name‐bearer|qq=|1=name‐bearer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}}, then the Nukatabe clan was already in charge of the Nukatabe domain, which is consistent.
One year later, it is recorded that Silla destroyed the Mimana government establishment.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇23rd year 1月条</ref>
Furthermore, according to volume 22 of the Shoki and the [[:en:Book of Sui|Suishu]], in 608 during Empress Suiko’s reign, {{ill|Nukatabe Hibarobu|ja|額田部比羅夫|lt=Nukatabe Ren Hibarobu|lt_ja=額田部連比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hibarobu|qq=|1=Nukatabe Hibarobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55050985}} (Nukatabe no Muraji Hibarobu) welcomed the Sui envoy, Pei Seisei ([[:en:Pei Shiqing|Pei Seisei]]), at {{ill|Kaiseikyūshi|ja|海柘榴市 (Yamato Province)|lt=Kaiseikyūshi|lt_ja=海石榴市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiseikyūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tsubakichi) (present‐day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai‐shi]], Kanaya), and delivered a formal greeting (“words of courtesy”).
In 610, Nukatabe Hibarobu also welcomed envoys from Silla and Mimana, and, together with {{ill|Zen Omi Daitomo|ja|膳大伴|lt=Zen Omi Daitomo|lt_ja=膳臣大伴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zen Omi Daitomo|qq=|1=Zen Omi Daitomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55536854}} (Kashiwade no Omi Ōtomo), he served as the head of Zōma (Kazariuma). In the following year, 611, during the medicinal animal hunting (kusurigari = young deer antlers, bagged horns) in Utano, he, together with {{ill|Awata Hosomem Omi|ja|粟田細目|lt=Awata Hosomem Omi|lt_ja=粟田細目臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awata Hosomem Omi|qq=|1=Awata no Hosome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55050998}}, served as the local administrator (kotori, meaning commander).<ref>『日本書紀』推古天皇19th year 5月5日条</ref>
In 645 (the first year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]]), Nukatabe Ren’s nephew became {{ill|Hōtō|ja|法頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtō|qq=|1=Hōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272314}}.<ref>『日本書紀』孝徳天皇 大化元年8月8日条</ref>
The Nukatabe Ren clan, during the time of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in 684, obtained the title of {{ill|Yatsuiro no Sei|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsuiro no Sei|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} and the ministerial [[:en:Kabane|surname]] of Sukune.<ref>『日本書紀』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条</ref> Furthermore, according to volume 1 of the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], a descendant believed to be {{ill|Nukatabe Hayashi|ja|額田部林|lt=Nukatabe Ren Hayashi|lt_ja=額田部連林|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hayashi|qq=|1=Nukatabe Hayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532738}} (Nukatabe no Muraji Hayashi) participated in the compilation of the Taihō Code.<ref>『続日本紀』文武天皇4th year 6月17日条</ref>
== Nukatabe Clan of Nagato Province == They are a family of the {{ill|Anamono Kuni‐zukuri|ja|穴門国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamono Kuni‐zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊浦郡|1=Toyoura district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7830939}}.
According to volume 13 of the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], in 740 (the 12th year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]]) during the rebellion led by {{ill|Fujiwara Hirosi|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hirosi|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}}, on September 21 an unsigned individual from the [[:en:Toyoura District, Yamaguchi|Toyoura District]] of Nagato Province, holding the Rank “Gai‐Sei Hachii jō”, namely {{ill|Nukatabe Hiromaro|ja|額田部広麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Hiromaro|qq=|1=Nukatabe Hiromaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55050993}}, led 40 elite soldiers, crossed the sea, and landed in Kyushu.<ref>『続日本紀』聖武天皇 天平十二年9月24日条</ref> According to volume 14 of the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 続日本紀}}, after the suppression of this rebellion, it appears that he was granted the surname {{ill|Naotachi|ja|直|lt=Naotachi|lt_ja=直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naotachi|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}} and was promoted from the Rank of “Gai‐Sei Hachii jō” to {{ill|Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|ja|外従五位下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外位|1=gei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430321}}.<ref name="#1">『続日本紀』聖武天皇 天平十三年閏3月5日条</ref> In 738 (the 10th year of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]]), he departed for [[:en:Heijō-kyō|Heijō‐kyō]] as the {{ill|Burenshi|ja|部領使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burenshi|qq=|1=Burenshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532745}} of the “Tanra” islanders ([[:en:Tamna|Tanra]]), passing through {{ill|Suō Province|ja|Suō Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suō Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} along the way.
According to volume 28 of the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 続日本紀}}, in April 767 (the 3rd year of [[:en:Tenpyō-jingo|Tenpyō Shingogō]]), {{ill|Nukatabe Sekimori|ja|額田部塞守|lt=Nukatabe Naosekimori|lt_ja=額田部直塞守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Sekimori|qq=|1=Nukatabe Sekimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406938}}, holding the Rank “Gai‐chū Shichii jō” and serving as the commander of the Toyoura group in Nagato Province, presented 1,000,000 mon in coin and 10,000 bundles of rice, for which he was awarded the Rank of {{ill|Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|ja|外従五位下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gai‐chū Go‐i ge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外位|1=gei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430321}} and appointed as the great administrator of Toyoura District.<ref>『続日本紀』称徳天皇 天平神護3rd year 4月29日条</ref>
== Nukatabe Yudaren and Nukatabe Kataren == In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shin‐sen Seishi Roku]], two ancestral traditions of the Nukatabe clan are recorded as follows:
* During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Yungyō]], a descendant of {{ill|Amatsumikage no Mikoto|ja|天御影命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsumikage no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天目一箇神|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}} was sent to {{ill|Satsuma Province|ja|Satsuma Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and subdued the [[:en:Hayato people|Hayabito]]. Upon his return, he presented a single horse; and because the horse’s forehead bore hair in the shape of a paddy field, the emperor was pleased and granted him the surname of {{ill|Nukatabe Yudaren|ja|額田部湯坐連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Yudaren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『新撰姓氏録』允恭天皇御世。被遣Satsuma Province 。平隼人。復奏之日。献御馬一匹。額有町形廻毛。天皇嘉之。賜姓額田部湯坐連也。</ref>
* During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Yungyō]], a descendant of the third‐generation descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ|1=Amatsuhikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940228}}, namely {{ill|Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto|ja|意富伊我都命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itomi Igatomo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, presented a horse whose forehead bore hair in the shape of a rice paddy; consequently, he was granted the surname of {{ill|Nukatabe Kataren|ja|額田部河田連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Kataren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>『新撰姓氏録』允恭天皇御世。献額田馬。天皇勅。此馬額如田町。仍賜姓額田部河田連也。</ref>
== Other Nukatabe Clans == In regional branches of the Nukatabe, {{ill|Nukatabe Soteme|ja|額田部蘇提売|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe Soteme|qq=|1=Nukatabe Soteme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55532744}} (ぬかたべ の そてめ), who was honored in February of the 2nd year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] (768) under Emperor Shōtoku, is well known.<ref name="#1"/>

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55532736]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nukatabe Clan''' (額田部氏) was an ancient Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]], originating from the {{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}} organization under the {{ill|Me|ja|名代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Me|qq=|1=Me|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414721}} system.
== Overview == === Ancestry === The Nukatabe Ren clan claimed descent from {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}} (referred to as Amatsuhikone no Mikoto in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]). According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (Left Capital, Kami Clan section), they were descendants of "Otowiigatsu no Mikoto" (意富伊我都命). This deity was one of those born from the [[:en:Ukehi|ukehi]] ritual between [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, as described in [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Volume 1. Additionally, the clan is mentioned as an ancestor of the {{ill|Onokuchi no Miyatsuko|ja|凡河内氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onokuchi no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ōshikōchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395444}} and {{ill|Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|ja|山背国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Yamashiro no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470858}}.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kamiyo Volume 1, Sixth Section.</ref> The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] also associates the Nukatabe clan with the Nukatabe Yuzaka Ren and others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
A theory suggests the clan's establishment reflects conflicts between the Yamato and Izumo regions.{{See|{{ill|Nukatabe|ja|額田部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe|qq=|1=Nukatabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55521387}}}}
=== Base of Operations === The clan's base was located in Nukata Village, {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|qq=|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}}, Yamato Province (modern [[:en:Yamatokōriyama|Yamatokoriyama]]), where they built their {{ill|ujidera|ja|氏寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ujidera|qq=|1=Clan temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547815}}, [[:en:Kakuan-ji|Kakuan-ji]]. The preserved "Yamato Province Nukata-ji Temple Site and Strip Village Map" at the [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] provides a rare depiction of temple layouts and estates during the Nara period, revealing the clan’s faith and burial practices.
== Archaeological Evidence == === Izumo Nukatabe Clan === The [[:en:Fudoki|Fudoki]] of {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentions the Nukatabe no Omi family in its records on [[:en:Ohara District, Shimane|Ōhara District]]. One notable entry describes a previous {{ill|Shōryō|ja|少領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 郡司|1=gunji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4152149}}, Nukatabe no Omi Oshishima, who built a temple.<ref>{{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ōhara District, Tsūji Section.</ref>
A significant archaeological discovery related to this clan is the iron sword with silver-inlaid inscriptions found in the Okadayama Kofun (Matsue City, Shimane Prefecture). This sword, discovered during preservation work in 1983, bears the inscription "各田卩臣," interpreted as "Nukatabe no Omi." It suggests a connection to local governance during the late 6th century.
=== Other Artifacts === Other artifacts from the Okadayama Kofun include mirrors, weapons, and horse gear, reflecting the clan's social and political significance. These findings support the theory that Nukatabe no Omi managed local estates as part of the Yamato government’s administrative system.
== Key Figures == * '''Nukatabe Hirafu''' (額田部比羅夫): Notable as a diplomat under Empress Suiko, Hirafu welcomed the Sui envoy, Pei Shiqing, at Tsubaki Market and performed ceremonial duties.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Empress Suiko, Year 16.</ref> * '''Nukatabe no Omi Rei''' (額田部連甥): Appointed as {{ill|Hōtō|ja|法頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōtō|qq=|1=Hōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272314}} during Emperor Kōtoku's reign (645 CE).<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Kōtoku, Year 1.</ref>
== Nukatabe Ren and Nukatabe Kawada Ren == The [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] includes two origin stories: * Nukatabe Yuzaka Ren received their surname after presenting a horse with a field-shaped marking to Emperor Ingyō. * Nukatabe Kawada Ren also earned their name for offering a similarly marked horse.<ref>[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Emperor Ingyō Section.</ref> == See Also == * [[:en:Bemin|Be System]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
